demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Our 3-month-old's scalp is covered in these thick, greasy yellow crusts that smell awful, and now the skin behind both his ears has gone bright red, is weeping clear fluid, and even bled a bit because he keeps rubbing his head on the sheets. He's crying all the time, barely sleeping, and not feeding well. We're really scared it's turned into something serious.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's suddenly got much worse over the last two days — the weeping and the bit of bleeding tonight really frightened us and he won't settle.

symptom_narrative.onset: the scalp crusting started about 4 weeks ago and was mild at first
symptom_narrative.duration: about 4 weeks, but a lot worse in the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: thick greasy yellow crusts on the top of the head, and red raw weeping skin in the folds behind his ears
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it looks really bad now and he seems so uncomfortable and itchy
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: top of his head, his forehead, his eyebrows, and behind both ears
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time, worse when he rubs his head
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: rubbing his head on the mattress seems to make the raw bits worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he's very irritable and crying a lot and feeding less than usual
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he's never had anything like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he can't sleep and is feeding poorly because he's so unsettled

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: first baby; parents have been applying natural oils to his scalp to try to soften the crusts

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: no concerns — he was well and growing normally before this

objective_data_offered.value: 36.8 degrees on our ear thermometer, so no fever

thick, waxy, yellow-brown crust across the top of his head; red flaky patches on his forehead and eyebrows; behind both ears the skin is bright red, shiny and weeping, with a tiny crack on the right side that bled a little
his colour looks normal and pink and he's breathing comfortably, not fast or struggling
no thick yellow or green pus, the redness hasn't spread onto his neck or chest, no vomiting; he is crying a lot, rubbing his head, and taking less milk than usual
